name: Beer-Lambert Quantitative Analysis
description: Quantitative absorption spectroscopy relies on the Beer-Lambert law A = epsilon*b*c: a wavelength near the absorption maximum is selected, absorbance is kept within the linear range by dilution, a calibration curve is built from standards, and the unknown concentration is interpolated from its measured absorbance.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Analyte in solution"]
    N2["Select lambda (max)"]
    N3["Measure absorbance A"]
    N4{"In linear range?"}
    N5["Dilute sample"]
    N6["Prepare standards"]
    N7["Build calibration curve"]
    N8["Fit A = epsilon b c"]
    N9["Interpolate unknown"]
    N10["Quantified concentration"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 -->|no| N5
    N5 -->|remeasure| N3
    N4 -->|yes| N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
